segments.text: Behold my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, in whom my soul delighteth; I have put my spirit upon him: he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Description of the servant's demeanor:
He shall not cry, nor lift up, nor cause his voice to be heard in the street.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: A bruised reed shall he not break, and the smoking flax shall he not quench: he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He shall not fail nor be discouraged, till he have set judgment in the earth: and the isles shall wait for his law.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thus saith God the LORD, he that created the heavens, and stretched them out; he that spread forth the earth, and that which cometh out of it; he that giveth breath unto the people upon it, and spirit to them that walk therein:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I the LORD have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: To open the blind eyes, to bring out the prisoners from the prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am the LORD: that is my name: and my glory will I not give to another, neither my praise to graven images.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Behold, the former things are come to pass, and new things do I declare: before they spring forth I tell you of them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Sing unto the LORD a new song, and his praise from the end of the earth, ye that go down to the sea, and all that is therein; the isles, and the inhabitants thereof.
segments.speaker: Unidentified Worshippers

segments.text: Let the wilderness and the cities thereof lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar doth inhabit: let the inhabitants of the rock sing, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
segments.speaker: Unidentified Worshippers

segments.text: Let them give glory unto the LORD, and declare his praise in the islands.
segments.speaker: Unidentified Worshippers

segments.text: The LORD shall go forth as a mighty man, he shall stir up jealousy like a man of war: he shall cry, yea, roar; he shall prevail against his enemies.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have long time holden my peace; I have been still, and refrained myself: now will I cry like a travailing woman; I will destroy and devour at once.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: devour: Heb. swallow, or, sup up

segments.text: I will make waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbs; and I will make the rivers islands, and I will dry up the pools.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I will bring the blind by a way that they knew not; I will lead them in paths that they have not known: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight. These things will I do unto them, and not forsake them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Judgment on idolaters:
They shall be turned back, they shall be greatly ashamed, that trust in graven images, that say to the molten images, Ye are our gods.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hear, ye deaf; and look, ye blind, that ye may see.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who is blind, but my servant? or deaf, as my messenger that I sent? who is blind as he that is perfect, and blind as the LORD's servant?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Description of spiritual blindness:
Seeing many things, but thou observest not; opening the ears, but he heareth not.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The LORD is well pleased for his righteousness' sake; he will magnify the law, and make it honourable.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Condition of the people:
But this is a people robbed and spoiled; they are all of them snared in holes, and they are hid in prison houses: they are for a prey, and none delivereth; for a spoil, and none saith, Restore.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: they are all…: or, in snaring all the young men of them
for a spoil: Heb. a treading

segments.text: Who among you will give ear to this? who will hearken and hear for the time to come?
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: for…: Heb. for the after time?

segments.text: Who gave Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to the robbers? did not the LORD, he against whom we have sinned? for they would not walk in his ways, neither were they obedient unto his law.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore he hath poured upon him the fury of his anger, and the strength of battle: and it hath set him on fire round about, yet he knew not; and it burned him, yet he laid it not to heart.
segments.speaker: God
